question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getNextBalanceSnapshot()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing the accountBalanceFile.getConsensusTimestamp() to be 0 or a very small value, trigger an arithmetic underflow in recordFileRepository.findNextBetween(timestamp - 1L, Long.MAX_VALUE) where timestamp - 1L wraps to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the query to return no results and silently skipping record file association?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: BalanceSnapshot.getTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing accountBalanceFile.getTimeOffset() to be a large positive value such that consensusTimestamp + timeOffset overflows a signed long, cause getTimestamp() to return a negative or incorrect timestamp that is used in CRYPTO_TRANSFER_QUERY and TOKEN_TRANSFER_QUERY, leading to incorrect reconciliation results or a permanent failure state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcileTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing a balance mismatch with ACCUMULATE strategy and millions of differing accounts, cause log.warn(error) to attempt to log a multi-gigabyte string, exhausting heap memory in the logging framework and crashing the importer service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcileTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing repeated balance mismatches across thousands of balance file pairs with ACCUMULATE strategy, cause the reconciliationJob.getError() string to grow to gigabytes in size, causing reconciliationJobRepository.save() in the finally block to attempt to persist a multi-gigabyte string to the database, causing a DB error or OOM?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing the reconciliation job to process thousands of balance files without errors (so count increments freely), cause the reconciliationJob.getCount() to overflow a long after Long.MAX_VALUE iterations, causing the count % 100 == 0 check to behave unexpectedly and potentially skip progress saves?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getLatestJob()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing the reconciliation_job table to accumulate a very large number of rows (since a new job is created on every run via reconciliationJobRepository.save()), cause the findLatest() query ('select * from reconciliation_job order by timestamp_start desc limit 1') to become slow due to missing or degraded index, causing a DB-level DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing the reconciliation service to process a very large number of balance files with ACCUMULATE strategy, cause the JVM to spend excessive time in garbage collection due to the large number of short-lived HashMap objects created per iteration (one per getAccountBalances() and getTokenBalances() call), leading to GC pauses that effectively halt the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAccountBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user, by submitting transactions that cause the account_balance table to have duplicate account_id entries for the same consensus_timestamp (if DB constraints allow it), cause the balances.put(accountId, balance) call to silently overwrite previous entries, causing the total.addAndGet(balance) to count the same account twice and fail the FIFTY_BILLION_HBARS check, permanently halting reconciliation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing the reconciliation service to process a very large number of balance files where each file has millions of token balances, cause the getTokenBalances() method to be called thousands of times each loading millions of rows, causing cumulative DB query load that exhausts DB connection pool resources?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing the reconciliation service to process a very large number of balance files where each file requires a separate BALANCE_QUERY, CRYPTO_TRANSFER_QUERY, TOKEN_BALANCE_QUERY, and TOKEN_TRANSFER_QUERY execution, cause the DB to be overwhelmed with sequential full-table-scan queries, causing a non-network DoS of the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcileCryptoTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user, by submitting a very large number of crypto transfers between two consecutive balance file timestamps, cause the CRYPTO_TRANSFER_QUERY GROUP BY aggregation to consume excessive DB CPU and memory, causing a DB-level resource exhaustion DoS that affects the entire mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcileTokenTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user, by submitting a very large number of token transfers between two consecutive balance file timestamps, cause the TOKEN_TRANSFER_QUERY GROUP BY aggregation to consume excessive DB CPU and memory, causing a DB-level resource exhaustion DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/reconciliation/BalanceReconciliationService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing the reconciliation service to process a very large number of balance files, cause the Stopwatch.createStarted() elapsed time to overflow or produce incorrect results, leading to misleading log output that masks a DoS condition?
